title: Code Review verdict: APPROVE — ADR-0002 @ 36baab9 (committed, 3 pure utils, 2 non-gating low notes)
description: Reviewed slug.ts/num.ts/str.ts against the live tree at commit 36baab9 (hash matched handoff, clean tree — the uncommitted-on-main work is now committed on the dev-clone branch). 25/25 green, tsc clean, zero cross-imports, zero call sites, diff ADR-0002-scoped — all verified independently. No security or correctness defects. Verdict APPROVE, consistent with prior human-approved reviews. Two low-severity notes are forward-looking (Unicode handling) for when these utils get wired to live data, not merge blockers.

title: Security re-scan — 2 Medium findings STILL OPEN (local-seo-agency @ 36baab9) — 12th scan
description: Re-scan at 36baab9 confirms both prior Medium findings remain unremediated (package.json overrides=NONE; page.tsx:66-67 dangerouslySetInnerHTML markup unchanged). 0 new security issues. ADR-0002 changeset (slug/num/str + tests) is pure utils, scanned clean. @google/generative-ai still unused. This is the 12th consecutive identical scan — strongly recommend consolidating into a single standing ticket.

title: Release v0.2.11 created locally — approve to publish + resolve recurring orphaned-tag drift
description: A PATCH release (0.2.0→0.2.11) was committed and tagged locally (commit 70b86e1, annotated tag v0.2.11). It adds three pure-utility lib modules (slug/num/str), a node --test harness (25 tests passing), and ADR/planning docs. Versioning skipped 0.2.1–0.2.10 because those tags persist on commits orphaned by twelve workspace resets. Decide whether to publish and how to resolve the recurring orphaned-tag drift.
